title: TC-FW-VERSION-001: Verify firmware version updates on Home screen immediately after DFU completes (no app restart)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Navigate to Home screen and note the current firmware version displayed (e.g., under device name or status section).
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and check for available firmware update.
If an update is available, tap the update banner or 'Update Now' button to start the DFU.
Confirm the update when prompted.
Do NOT use the device during the update. Wait for the update to complete (device will restart automatically).
After the device restarts and reconnects, observe the firmware version displayed on the Home screen.
Do NOT close or restart the app at any point.
expected: The firmware version displayed on the Home screen updates to the new version immediately after the DFU completes and the device reconnects, without requiring an app restart.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-002: Verify firmware version updates on Device Settings page immediately after DFU completes (no app restart)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Navigate to Home screen and note the current firmware version.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and note the firmware version displayed there.
Start a firmware update (DFU) by tapping the update banner or 'Update Now'.
Confirm the update and wait for it to complete (device restarts automatically).
After the device reconnects, navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates.
Observe the firmware version displayed on this page.
Do NOT close or restart the app at any point.
expected: The firmware version displayed on the Device Settings → Software & Updates page updates to the new version immediately after the DFU completes and the device reconnects, without requiring an app restart.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-003: Verify stale firmware version persists across app navigation (Home → Device Settings) after DFU
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Note the current firmware version on the Home screen (e.g., version A).
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and confirm the same version (A) is displayed.
Perform a firmware update (DFU) and wait for completion and device reboot.
After the device reconnects, navigate from Home screen to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates.
Observe the firmware version on the Software & Updates page.
Navigate back to the Home screen and observe the firmware version there.
Repeat navigation between Home screen and Device Settings → Software & Updates two more times.
Do NOT close or restart the app.
expected: The firmware version is consistently updated to the new version on both the Home screen and Device Settings → Software & Updates page across all navigations, with no stale version data persisting.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-004: Verify app restart is NOT required to see updated firmware version after DFU
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Record the current firmware version displayed on the Home screen (e.g., version 1.0.0).
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and initiate a firmware update.
Wait for the DFU to complete and the device to reboot and reconnect.
Without restarting the app, check the firmware version on the Home screen and record it.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and record the firmware version.
Force-close the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe it away from the recent apps list).
Reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app and wait for the device to reconnect.
Check the firmware version on the Home screen and on Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates.
Compare all recorded firmware versions.
expected: The firmware version displayed before the app restart (steps 5-6) matches the firmware version displayed after the app restart (step 9), confirming that an app restart is NOT required to see the updated firmware version after DFU.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-005: Verify firmware version updates on Home screen after DFU with app backgrounded during update
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Note the current firmware version on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and start a firmware update.
After confirming the update, press the Home button to background the app (do NOT force-close it).
Wait for the DFU to complete (device will restart).
After waiting at least 30 seconds post-reboot, reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app from the recent apps list.
Observe the firmware version displayed on the Home screen after the app resumes.
expected: The firmware version on the Home screen updates to the new version after the app resumes from background following DFU completion, without requiring a full app restart.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-006: Verify firmware version updates on Device Settings page after DFU with app backgrounded during update
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and note the current firmware version.
Start a firmware update from the Software & Updates page.
After confirming the update, press the Home button to background the app.
Wait for the DFU to complete and the device to reboot.
After 30 seconds, reopen the app from the recent apps list.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and observe the firmware version.
expected: The firmware version on the Device Settings → Software & Updates page updates to the new version after the app resumes from background following DFU completion, without requiring a full app restart.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-007: Verify firmware version updates on Home screen after DFU with app force-killed and reopened
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Note the current firmware version on the Home screen (e.g., version X).
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and start a firmware update.
After confirming the update, immediately force-close the app (swipe away from recent apps).
Wait for the DFU to complete (device will reboot).
After the device has rebooted and is idle, reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the device to reconnect automatically.
Observe the firmware version displayed on the Home screen.
expected: The firmware version on the Home screen displays the new version after the app is reopened following DFU completion, confirming that the app correctly reads the updated firmware from the device on fresh launch.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-008: Verify firmware version updates on Device Settings page after DFU with app force-killed and reopened
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and note the current firmware version.
Start a firmware update from the Software & Updates page.
After confirming the update, immediately force-close the app.
Wait for the DFU to complete and the device to reboot.
Reopen the Poly Studio Mobile app and wait for reconnection.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and observe the firmware version.
expected: The firmware version on the Device Settings → Software & Updates page displays the new version after the app is reopened following DFU completion.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-009: Verify firmware version consistency across Home screen and Device Settings after DFU with rapid navigation
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Note the firmware version on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and note the version.
Start a firmware update and wait for completion and device reboot.
After reconnection, rapidly navigate: Home screen → Device Settings → Software & Updates → Home screen → Device Settings → Software & Updates (3 full cycles within 10 seconds).
On each screen visit, observe the displayed firmware version.
expected: The firmware version is consistently and correctly displayed as the new version on every screen visit during rapid navigation, with no stale version appearing on any screen.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-010: Verify firmware version updates after DFU when device is disconnected and reconnected post-update
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Note the current firmware version on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and start a firmware update.
Wait for the DFU to complete and the device to reboot.
After the device reconnects to the app, manually disconnect the device (turn off Bluetooth or put device out of range).
Wait 10 seconds, then reconnect the device (turn Bluetooth back on or bring device in range).
Wait for the app to show the device as connected.
Observe the firmware version on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and observe the firmware version.
expected: The firmware version on both the Home screen and Device Settings → Software & Updates page displays the new version after the device is disconnected and reconnected post-DFU, without requiring an app restart.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-011: Verify firmware version updates after DFU when phone is rebooted before checking
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Note the current firmware version on the Home screen (e.g., version A).
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and start a firmware update.
Wait for the DFU to complete and the device to reboot.
After the device reconnects to the app, reboot the phone (power off and on).
After phone restarts, open the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the device to connect automatically.
Observe the firmware version on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and observe the firmware version.
expected: The firmware version on both the Home screen and Device Settings → Software & Updates page displays the new version after a phone reboot, confirming no stale cached version persists across phone reboots.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-012: Verify firmware version updates after DFU when device is powered off and on before checking
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Note the current firmware version on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and start a firmware update.
Wait for the DFU to complete and the device to reboot.
After the device reconnects to the app, power off the device completely.
Wait 10 seconds, then power on the device.
Wait for the device to reconnect to the app automatically.
Observe the firmware version on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and observe the firmware version.
expected: The firmware version on both the Home screen and Device Settings → Software & Updates page displays the new version after the device is power-cycled post-DFU, without requiring an app restart.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-013: Verify firmware version updates after DFU with slider adjustment immediately after update
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Note the current firmware version on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and start a firmware update.
Wait for the DFU to complete and the device to reboot.
After reconnection, immediately navigate to Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment.
Set Surround level to 75.
Navigate back to the Home screen and observe the firmware version.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and observe the firmware version.
Verify that the Surround level is still set to 75.
expected: The firmware version is correctly updated on both the Home screen and Device Settings page after DFU, AND the Surround level setting of 75 persists correctly, confirming that firmware version refresh does not corrupt other settings.

title: TC-FW-VERSION-014: Verify firmware version updates after DFU when app is updated between DFU and version check
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly Bluetooth audio device.
Note the current firmware version on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and start a firmware update.
Wait for the DFU to complete and the device to reboot.
After the device reconnects, check if there is an app update available in the Google Play Store / Apple App Store.
If an app update is available, update the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open the updated app and wait for the device to reconnect.
Observe the firmware version on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] → Software & Updates and observe the firmware version.
expected: The firmware version on both the Home screen and Device Settings page displays the new version after the app is updated, confirming that the app correctly reads the device firmware version on first connection after an app update.
